name: Cato's Cheese Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Feta cheese, drained, crumbled, and packed into the measuring cup
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the cheese into a medium-sized mixing bowl. Mash well with your fingers until it becomes a smooth, lump-free paste. Add the flour and mix well with your fingers. Add the beaten egg and mix well. The dough will be rather sticky.
2. Divide the dough into two equal parts. Form two round, flat, 1/2 inch thick loaves. Lay each on three bay leaves set on a greased baking sheet, and bake in a preheated 400 degree oven for 1 to 1-1/2 hours, or until cooked through.
3. Remove the loaves from the oven. Spread the tops with honey, and let cool. Remove the bay leaves before serving. NOTE: If Feta cheese is unavailable, use 1 cup cottage cheese and add 1/4 cup extra flour (but the taste is inferior).
